free_text: Post-neutron mass distribution for Pu-238(n,f)
CEN-Grenoble Melusine reactor and
Grenoble high-flux reactor.
15 min and 2 hr irradiations were performed in-
side a 1 m3 D2O container near the Melusine reactor
core. The neutron flux was 1.6 x 10**13 n/cm2sec and
the thermal/epithermal ratio was 2.5 x 10**3.
90 sec irradiations were done in the high-flux
reactor with a neutron flux of 10.**14 n/cm2/sec and
a thermal/epithermal ratio of 5.0 x 10.**3.
Thermal reactor spectrum.
An energy- and efficiency-calibrated 63 cm3 high
purity germanium detector was used to count and follow
the decay of the different gamma-ray lines to identify
the fission products.
0.6 micrograms of Pu-238 was placed in a polyethylene
capsule of 7 mm diameter and 15 mm height, covered
with a 2 mm thick layer of special glue. A lid of
similar material was sealed to the top of the capsule
to ensure that no gaseous fission products escape from
the capsule.
Also blank capsules were used to ensure that no con-
taminants from U-235 were present.
The data were corrected for dead time.
No information about error source.
N.O.
S.W. Minor BIB correction & critique added
in subentry 2.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
DECAY-DATA corrected in Subent 003; STATUS moved to
Subent 001. Cosmetic corrections. Subent 005 added.
.The t1/2 entered for nuclide 17 in the list of decay-
data and taken from the main ref is not appropriate
for 45-Rh-109, and neither is the '-g' since no meta-
stable state exists for this nuclide. However, the dg
value is correct. It is possible that the author
intended 44-Ru-109-g which has a t1/2 of 34.5 sec, but
the dg value would then not correspond (should be
S.W. Critique added.
SD: Sb-130m -> Sb-130g in DECAY-DATA;
corresponding value of ISOMER was corrected.
SD
